meta.feed_title: World News Today: International News Headlines - The Hindu | The Hindu

items.title: Thailand court acquits progressive political leader Thanathorn Juangroongruangkit on charges of royal defamation
items.summary: A Bangkok court has acquitted former Future Forward Party leader Thanathorn Juangroongruangkit in a rare ruling under Thailand’s strict lese majeste law

items.title: Indian national living illegally in U.S. held by Federal authorities for 'assaulting man with baseball bat'
items.summary: An Indian national accused of assaulting a man with a baseball bat in Massachusetts has been arrested by U.S. immigration authorities after local officials allegedly released him without notifying ICE

items.title: Jaishankar, Ukrainian FM discuss war, peace efforts amid evolving battlefield situation
items.summary: India has consistently called for dialogue and diplomacy to resolve the Ukraine conflict while maintaining close relations with both Russia and Ukraine

items.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: U.S., Iran trade strikes in most serious clash since truce began
items.summary: The mixed signals have thrown into question talks aimed at formally ending the war that began on February 28 with U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran

items.title: Italy declares red heatwave alert for Rome, three other cities
items.summary: Italy's Health Ministry issued the alert to warn the cities of Bologna, Florence, Rome and Turin and advised the public to stay out of the sun

items.title: Russia signs agreement to build first nuclear power plant in Kazakhstan
items.summary: The two sides ​also signed an agreement on the provision of a Russian export ⁠credit to finance the construction of the plant

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant gets more than three years in prison for central role in his ketamine death
items.summary: Mathew Perry became a major TV star along with Courteney Cox, Jennifer Aniston, Matt LeBlanc, David Schwimmer and Lisa Kudrow on “Friends”, NBC's megahit sitcom that ran from 1994 to 2004

items.title: Australian PM Anthony Albanese hails India’s economic growth; looks forward to hosting PM Modi again
items.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese praised the growing India-Australia relationship and said he looked forward to welcoming Prime Minister Narendra Modi to Australia again soon

items.title: Russia will not choose who speaks for Europe in potential Ukraine talks, EU ministers say
items.summary: The European Ministers stressed the need for a unified EU stance before any negotiations with Moscow

items.title: A journey of the heart: One Sri Lankan officer’s transformative train adventure through India
items.summary: Forty days, some 13,000 kilometres, km, and 24 trains crisscrossing India — that sums up Saman Athaudahetti’s February and March, as he chased his long-held dream of exploring this neighbouring country through its railway network

items.title: Trump appears to threaten to ‘blow up’ ally Oman over Strait of Hormuz control
items.summary: The U.S. President said Oman must "behave" or he would "blow them up," when he was asked if he would accept a short-term deal to allow Iran and the Gulf state to control the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: NASA lays out moon base plans with landers, buggies and drones
items.summary: For next year's Artemis III, another team of astronauts will practise docking NASA's Orion capsule in orbit around Earth with the lunar landers being developed for crews by Blue Origin and Elon Musk's SpaceX

items.title: India's energy investment to hit $170 billion in 2026 on solar, grid and refining push: International Energy Agency
items.summary: ‘Investment in solar PV grew annually by 25% in this period, and oil refining by 23%. Together, these two sectors contributed to one-fourth of India's energy investment growth,’ said the International Energy Agency

items.title: Two Indian peacekeepers to be honoured posthumously on International Peacekeepers Day
items.summary: Nearly 180 Indian peacekeepers have made the supreme sacrifice in the line of duty, the highest number by far from any troop-contributing country

items.title: SpaceX's Starship rockets grounded pending investigation after test flight
items.summary: The spacecraft continued around the world, releasing 20 mock satellites before ending the mission as planned with a fiery splashdown in the Indian Ocean

items.title: U.S. envoy warns Spain to be 'very careful' in deepening ties with China
items.summary: U.S. Ambassador Benjamin Leon cautioned Spain against allowing Chinese companies greater access to strategic sectors such as telecommunications and defence

items.title: U.S. military strikes another alleged drug boat in eastern Pacific, killing two
items.summary: U.S. Southern Command posted a video on social media showing a boat resting on the water before being struck by an explosion

items.title: Iran forces fired at four ships attempting to cross Hormuz: state broadcaster
items.summary: “Four vessels attempted to cross the Strait of Hormuz and enter the Persian Gulf without coordination with the security forces,” IRIB posted on Telegram

items.title: Iran says war unlikely as Trump warns he may ‘finish the job’
items.summary: Iran and the U.S. have traded threats for weeks while negotiating through Pakistani mediation

items.title: U.S. military conducts another defensive strike against Iran

items.title: U.S. Military carries out ‘self-defence’ strikes in Iran, including on missile launch sites
items.summary: The strikes were done “to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces”, but the military was “using restraint during the ongoing ceasefire”

items.title: Iran-Israel war highlights: U.S. military strike in eastern Pacific kills two men
items.summary: Iran says war unlikely as U.S. President Donald Trump warns he may 'finish the job'

items.title: Trump says U.S. not satisfied yet on deal with Iran
items.summary: Iran is very ​much ‌intent, they want very much to make a deal. ‌So far they ​haven’t gotten there ... we’re ⁠not satisfied with it, but we will ‌be, says U.S. President Donald Trump

items.title: Tibet’s exiled government leader sworn in
items.summary: The government's ‘sikyong’, or leader, Penpa Tsering, was elected for a second term, after taking 61% in the preliminary round, a high enough threshold to win outright.

items.title: Trump Board of Peace's official Gaza fund is empty
items.summary: The source said money had not been deposited because the fund was designed for the reconstruction and development phase, which has not yet been reached.

items.title: Watch: ED raids on Pinarayi: Why the Left alleges a Congress-BJP nexus | Above the Fold | 27.05.2026
items.summary: In today’s episode, we look at the ED raids linked to former Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan, the latest Karnataka leadership shuffle developments, and the SC upholding the SIR process, to U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham’s remarks on Pakistan’s mediation role, and more.

items.title: Spain PM keeps backing ex-leader targeted in graft probe
items.summary: The affair is part of a growing list of graft cases that have raised questions about the viability of Mr. Sanchez's minority Socialist government and contributed to a string of heavy regional election defeats.

items.title: Iran says U.S., Israel goal still to overthrow Islamic republic
items.summary: The Ministry said it received intelligence that the U.S. and Israel would seek to "intensify economic pressure", create divisions in the country among religious and ethnic groups and carry out sabotage and other "terrorist operations".

items.title: WHO warns of 'catastrophic collision' of Ebola and war in DR Congo
items.summary: Neighbouring Uganda, which has recorded seven cases of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola responsible for the outbreak, announced it was shutting its border with the DRC with immediate effect.

items.title: Hezbollah says clashed with Israeli troops in south Lebanon beyond 'yellow line'
items.summary: Israel stepped up strikes on south and east Lebanon on Tuesday (May 26), issuing evacuation warnings for at least 50 towns and villages and killing at least 31 people, as Hezbollah also kept up its attacks.

items.title: Uganda closes its border with Congo as cases of a rare Ebola type surge
items.summary: Ugandan authorities have ordered the closure of the border with Congo amid a surge in Ebola cases

items.title: China asks Quad to stop meddling in maritime affairs in its neighbourhood
items.summary: Certain countries should stop meddling in maritime affairs in China's neighbourhood and earnestly respect regional countries’ efforts to safeguard peace and stability, says Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning

items.title: Hungary's Parliament votes to remain member of International Criminal Court
items.summary: The Hungarian Parliament has backed legislation maintaining the country’s membership in the International Criminal Court, the world’s permanent tribunal for war crimes and genocide

items.title: Fire at London kosher store in arson-hit area not ruled 'suspicious'
items.summary: Emergency crews responded to a major fire at a kosher supermarket in north London on Wednesday amid heightened concerns following recent attacks targeting Jewish community sites

items.title: Bangladesh probes hospital deaths of six newborns
items.summary: Health authorities in Bangladesh are investigating the deaths of six newborns at a private hospital in Dhaka amid reports of ventilation and air-conditioning failure

items.title: Israel says it killed new Hamas military leader in Gaza
items.summary: Family members of a man called Mohammed Odeh said he was killed in the strike, but did not confirm he was the leader of the military wing. Hamas has not commented

items.title: Pope Leo decries 'sharp intensification' of war in Ukraine
items.summary: ‘I am following with concern the war in ‌Ukraine,’ Leo, the first ​U.S. Pope, said

items.title: Philippine bishop, ex-ICC judge lead new inquiry into thousands of Duterte-era killings
items.summary: Raul Pangalangan, a Philippine lawyer and ex-ICC judge, said the commission he will head ‘was created to ensure that the stories of victims, survivors and families are heard, verified and preserved’

items.title: Russian lawmakers want banks, their staff to help fight Ukrainian drones
items.summary: Russia’s lower house of Parliament has approved a bill allowing banks to install anti-drone systems and authorise selected employees to counter incoming Ukrainian drones

items.title: Chinese dissident escapes to South Korea by rubber boat
items.summary: The reported escape of veteran Chinese activist Dong Guangping to South Korea has drawn international attention to the challenges faced by political dissidents seeking asylum

items.title: Human Rights Watch says U.S. deported thousands of Cubans, Venezuelans to danger in Mexico
items.summary: More than half of the Cubans deported had a criminal record, but only 16% were for violent crimes, according to the researchers and one-fourth had no criminal history

items.title: U.S. military strike on alleged drug boat in eastern Pacific kills one, leaves two survivors
items.summary: The Pentagon watchdog said last week that it will evaluate whether the U.S. military followed an established targeting framework when carrying out the attacks on alleged drug-smuggling boats

items.title: Sri Lanka parliamentary poll: President Anura’s ruling coalition wins big
items.summary: National People’s Power wins 159 seats in 225-member House, securing two-thirds majority; the historic sweep also includes impressive wins in the north and east that are home to Tamils and Muslims

items.title: Panama ties should not be subject to third-party interference, says China
items.summary: China and Hong Kong authorities have said they oppose the ruling against CK Hutchison's port concessions, calling ‌it an ‘act of bad faith’

items.title: China supports 'active mediation' by Pakistan between U.S.-Iran, says Foreign Minister Wang Yi
items.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi’s remarks came as U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham said in a post on X that Pakistan, as a ‘mediator’ between the U.S. and Iran, is more than ‘problematic’

items.title: At least 1 dead, 9 others missing in chemical tank implosion at Washington State mill
items.summary: The facility, which employs about 1,000 people, makes material for tissues, printing paper, cups, plates, cartons and other goods

items.title: Donald Trump declares himself in ‘perfect’ health, as 80th birthday looms
items.summary: ‘Everything checked out PERFECTLY,’ posted U.S. President Donald Trump as he drove back to the White House from the Walter Reed Military Hospital near Washington

items.title: Pakistan's role as mediator problematic: U.S. senator
items.summary: Lindsey Graham asked Pakistan to respond to U.S. President Donald Trump's call to mediators in the U.S.-Iran war to join the Accords.

items.title: Why Trump's Abraham Accords expansion push faces resistance
items.summary: Donald Trump’s push to expand the Abraham Accords amid ongoing regional conflict has exposed the widening gap between U.S. diplomatic ambitions and political realities in West Asia

items.title: Three dead in latest strike, Israel claims Hamas military chief was targetted
items.summary: Airstrikes targeted the new leader of Hamas' military wing, Mohammed Odeh, says Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defence Minister Israel Katz in joint statement

items.title: Under U.S. pressure, Cuba appeals for international help
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has openly mused about taking over communist Cuba, saying that after the U.S. attack in January that ousted Nicolas Maduro in Venezuela, Havana could be his next target for military action and big change

items.title: Canada imposes Ebola-related travel ban, Bahamas to increase screening
items.summary: Canadian citizens, permanent residents and other foreign nationals who have been in affected areas in ⁠recent weeks and do not have symptoms will have to quarantine for 21 days from May 30, ‌according to a statement from Canada’s public health agency

items.title: Iran-Israel war highlights: Gulf ⁠powers will ⁠no longer be ​a shield ‌for U.S. bases, Iran's supreme leader says
items.summary: Israel’s military warned residents of 10 villages, most of them in southern Lebanon, on Monday to evacuate their homes ahead of expected strikes against alleged Hezbollah targets

items.title: Gaza health officials say 7 killed in Israeli airstrikes
items.summary: The Israeli military said its troops identified several “armed terrorists” operating in the area of the so-called “Yellow Line” that splits Gaza into two areas: one controlled by Israeli forces and the other by Hamas

items.title: Turkish riot police use water cannons ahead of deposed Opposition leader's speech to rally
items.summary: Ozgur Ozel and the core leadership of the Republican People's Party, or CHP, were removed from their posts on May 26 by a court order that many people consider to be politically motivated

items.title: China, Pakistan agree to 'unswervingly safeguard' bilateral ties
items.summary: Statement was issued at the end of Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif's official visit to China from May 23-26 at invitation of  Premier Li Qiang. During his visit, two sides signed a number of cooperation documents in various fields

items.title: North Korea launches ballistic missile, other weapons over sea in latest show of force
items.summary: The missile fired from Jongju, a city near the North’s west coast, flew about 80 kilometres (50 miles), South Korea’s Joint Chiefs of Staff said. North Korea launched other kinds of projectiles, it said, but didn’t elaborate

items.title: Iran accuses U.S. of breaking truce after new strikes
items.summary: Iranian state media reported overnight blasts in the southern port city of Bandar Abbas, near the Strait of Hormuz, and the country’s Revolutionary Guards said its forces had downed a U.S. drone entering its airspace and had fired at an F-35 fighter jet

items.title: Israeli strike on village in eastern Lebanon kills 12, as Israel calls up more troops there
items.summary: The strike hit the village of Mashghara in the Bekaa Valley late May 25, according to the media reports

items.summary: From the QUAD meeting in New Delhi and India-U.S. critical minerals deal to fresh AIADMK defections to the TVK and rising CNG prices - here are the top stories of the day.
